tweets.text: SMOKE SHOW: @CBPAMO Agents coordinated with international partners to take down 7,000 lbs. of marijuana in international waters. An Air and Marine Operations MQ-9 crew spotted the go-fast type vessel loaded with narcotics. The interdiction resulted in the seizure of over 3,000 packages and the arrest of 5 individuals. CBP is committed to preventing drugs from entering the United States by sea with the Coast Guard, the Department of War, and our international partners.
tweets.text_resolved: SMOKE SHOW: @CBPAMO Agents coordinated with international partners to take down 7,000 lbs. of marijuana in international waters. An Air and Marine Operations MQ-9 crew spotted the go-fast type vessel loaded with narcotics. The interdiction resulted in the seizure of over 3,000 packages and the arrest of 5 individuals. CBP is committed to preventing drugs from entering the United States by sea with the Coast Guard, the Department of War, and our international partners.

tweets.text: A Border Patrol K9 uncovered 32 pounds of cocaine hidden inside a floor compartment in a vehicle during a Border Patrol Checkpoint inspection in Tucson Sector. Our K9 partners play a crucial role in our mission of stopping illegal drugs from ending and ruining the lives of our citizens. Border security is national security!

tweets.text:
VEHICLE STOP ➡️ DEADLY DRUGS  

Border Patrol and the Orange County Sheriff's Office stopped a vehicle in San Clemente, leading to the seizure of $250K worth of fentanyl. We found 9 bundles weighing 22 lbs. concealed inside the center console. The driver was arrested and booked into the local jail. CBP and our local law enforcement work together to keep fentanyl off our streets.
tweets.text_resolved:

CBP and our local law enforcement work together to keep fentanyl off our streets.

tweets.text: "Historic security did not happen by accident, it's a result of the comprehensive approach to border security. We ended catch and release, we prioritize detention and removal authorities, and we sent a clear message that illegal entry into the United States will not be tolerated, and the numbers speak for themselves." @CBPCommissioner on our efforts to make the border the most secure it's been in history.

tweets.text: .@DFOTucson officers seized 412.70 lbs of meth and 53.35 lbs of fentanyl pills hidden within vehicles on an inbound rail auto carrier. A train with 118 rail cars was inspected during enforcement, revealing narcotics hidden in one of the auto carrier cars. Every pound seized is a life potentially saved.

tweets.text:
Keeping America safe extends beyond our borders. Securing large sporting events like the @SuperBowl is one of the many ways we are on the front lines safeguarding our nation. Check out some of the mission critical operations that we conduct to make sure everyone is safe at the biggest game of the year. #CBPxSBLX #CBPBeyondTheBorder #SuperBowlLX 
Learn more about our efforts: https://t.co/fnz18eCBd2

tweets.text:
Even Santa has made his travel plans easy and simplified! Join Santa in making your travel plans easy by using Global Entry! It allows expedited clearance for pre-approved travelers upon arrival in the United States. Members enter the country by accessing the Global Entry processing technology at selected airports.

Learn more about the program here: https://t.co/zrj5rp2kMF

tweets.text:
The CBP Home app is your chance to go home for the holidays, with an updated $3,000 stipend. If you're in the United States illegally, take this chance to leave voluntarily. 

Use it now and self-deport—or you will be found and deported with zero chance of returning.

CBP Home App Information ➡️ https://t.co/gKhHtJZKL9

tweets.text: At CBP we want to send our warmest wishes for a joyful and safe Christmas to all families across the nation. We extend the highest gratitude to our selfless workforce who remain dedicated to the mission. Your commitment to the mission inspires us all, and we thank you for your sacrifice and steadfast efforts to keep America safe.

tweets.text:
Our “Se Busca Información” initiative has new suspects, and we need your help to find them. You can confidentially report any information via WhatsApp or by calling 830-313-4557. Our phone line is open 24 hours, and calls go directly to the Border Patrol’s Texas call center. We share information with law enforcement in the U.S. and Mexico. The bilateral initiative between the U.S. and Mexico is in best interest of both countries to promote a safer and more secure shared border region.

Learn more: https://t.co/uCHsaGvJDV

tweets.text:
This holiday season, keep the family together while going through customs with Mobile Passport Control. Now one family member can submit the entire family's travel information through the app and you can speak to a CBP officer together.

Yes, it's free! 

The benefits of MPC are:
- Shorter wait times in Customs
- Free, easy-to-use app
- Groups up to 12 can be processed together
- Ability to declare items in app

More: https://t.co/Gfo9sHU6dT

tweets.text:
While many families are enjoying time together during the holidays, CBP officers are hard at work safeguarding the nation.

On Thanksgiving in 2022, CBP officers intercepted 26lbs of meth and 3lbs of fentanyl during an encounter with a Mexican citizen arriving to the U.S. from Mexico.

Thank you to the dedicated agents and officers who stay hard at work stopping dangerous drugs, criminals, goods, and more from entering through the border.

Read more: https://t.co/HrC6brpbpu

tweets.text:
Virginia CBP officers seized a shipment of counterfeit children's toys arriving from China. The shipment consisted of 17,634 pieces of children's products with an estimated value of over $776K had they been authentic. Counterfeit goods are usually made with parts that could break prematurely or cause harm to consumers. Remember to purchase from reputable retailers.

Read more: https://t.co/P4pBdZNvbr

tweets.text:
Two separate human smuggling events resulted in the arrests of 13 individuals, including two U.S. citizens. In both events, @USBPChiefTCA initiated vehicle stops, where the drivers attempted to flee, resulting in high-speed pursuits that ended in vehicle rollovers. Smugglers endanger their own lives and the safety of those they exploit and the public. We will continue to hold smugglers accountable and protect our borders.

Read more: https://t.co/zmHCrTFWWE

CBP officers in Puerto Rico seized 1,168 lbs of cocaine during three interdictions aboard a cargo vessel arriving from the Dominican Republic. A routine inspection was conducted revealing the narcotics in modified chassis compartments and duffle bags hidden within palletized cargo, with an estimated street value exceeding $10M.

Read more ➡️ https://go.dhs.gov/iXk
